mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0% over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and consumer credit balances indicates broad-based demand expansion that lifts cyclical equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained peak and subsequent decline in the industrial production index signals supply chain bottlenecks resolving into overcapacity, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between INDPRO declines and SPX returns exceeds 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, causing immediate positive repricing of equity indices over the next 5 trading days following each data release.
failure_condition: The sign of the forward return consistently contradicts the direction of GDPC1 acceleration in more than 50% of quarterly observation windows over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate raises borrowing costs and contracts liquidity, forcing equity risk premium expansion and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in 3 months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered tightening episodes, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.10 over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and rising unemployment claims indicates structural labor market stress that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 150k while UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in the same month
failure_condition: The joint divergence signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 45% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term p-value exceeds 0.10 in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in consumer credit balances alongside rising housing starts confirms robust household balance sheet expansion, which supports discretionary spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional correlation between the composite demand signal and index returns drops below 0.05 over a 2-year test.
